macro.title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Nasdaq Rise to New Records; Oil Gains as Trump Rejects 'Unacceptable' Iran Peace Plan Response - Investopedia
macro.summary: Major stock indexes advanced to begin the week, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite setting fresh records. Meanwhile, oil prices rose after President Donald Trump called Iran's peace proposal response "totally unacceptable."

macro.title: Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang Excluded from Trump’s China Trip - TipRanks
macro.summary: The chipmaker is effectively shut out of China.

macro.title: Why Micron Stock Keeps Going Up - The Motley Fool
macro.summary: What goes up must come down -- eventually.

macro.title: 'Atlanta Journal-Constitution' chief steps down as bold goals yield to tough realities - NPR
macro.summary: The owners of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution invested $150 million to reinvent the paper. The changes have been significant. Three years in, the payoff has been modest.

macro.title: Michael Burry is not a believer: 'For any stocks going parabolic reduce positions almost entirely' - CNBC
macro.summary: Michael Burry said investors should "reject greed" as enthusiasm around artificial intelligence and momentum-driven trades pushes valuations sharply higher.
